zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Ashe de Esse於1360年出生在英國戴文索福頓時，他的父親Henry Ashe de Esse33歲，母親Margery Fornyson20歲。他於1385年與Anne Geere在英國結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於1458年在他的家鄉享年98歲，埋葬在英國戴文索福頓。

nameMeanings.name: Ashe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish and English: variant of   Ash   1, found mainly in Ireland.
Americanized form (translation into English) of French   Dufresne  , a variant of   Ash   2.

History: This is the name of a prominent NC family; John Baptist Ashe settled in NC before 1720. It is not known where he came from: his personal name suggests that he may have been of French extraction, perhaps originally named Jean-Baptiste Dufresne.

variants.name: Ash
meanings: English: from Middle English asche ‘ash tree’ (Old English æsc), hence a topographic name for someone living by an ash tree or a habitational name from any of the many places in southern and central England named with this word (Derbyshire, Dorset, Hampshire, Herefordshire, Kent, Surrey, Shropshire, Somerset, and elsewhere).
Americanized form of German   Asch   and   Esch  , the latter ultimately also of Swiss German origin (see   Oesch  ).
Americanized form (translation into English) of French   Dufresne  , with the same meaning as 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Esse
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik.
Norwegian: habitational name from the farmstead Ese in Sogn of unexplained etymology, or Esse in Telemark, named with Old Norse æsin ‘hill’ + -e from vin ‘meadow’.
German: variant of   Ess  , or a topographic name from the river name Esse in Hesse.

variants.name: Ess
meanings: North German: topographic name for someone living on or owning land that was waterlogged or partly surrounded by water, from Middle Low German es ‘swamp, water’.
Swiss German: from the topographic name Ess ‘grazing area, pasture’.
Swiss German: from a vernacular short form of the personal name   Oswald  .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Ashe de Esse was born in 1360, in Sowton, Devon, England, his father, Henry Ashe de Esse, was 33 and his mother, Margery Fornyson, was 20. He married Anne Geere in 1385, in England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died in 1458, in his hometown, at the age of 98, and was buried in Sowton, Devon, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Ashe d’Esse est né en 1360, à Sowton, Devon, Angleterre, son père, Henry Ashe d’Esse, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Margery Fornyson, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Anne Geere en 1385, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Il est décédé en 1458, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 98 ans, et a été enterré à Sowton, Devon, Angleterre.

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Ashe de Esse(이)가 1360년 영국 데폰 소우턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Henry Ashe de Esse(은)는 33세였고, 그의 어머니 Margery Fornyson(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1385년 영국에서 Anne Geere(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1458년 그의 고향에서 98세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 데폰 소우턴에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Ashe de Esse родился в 1360 г. в местности соутон, девон, Англия, его отцу по имени Henry Ashe de Esse было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Margery Fornyson, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Anne Geere в 1385 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он умер в 1458 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 98 лет и был похоронен в местности соутон, девон, Англия.
